headline: Why The Narrative Around Alarm.com (ALRM) Is Shifting As Analysts Recut Valuation Targets
summary: Alarm.com Holdings is back in focus after a trim in its assessed fair value, from US$68.71 to US$58.00. This shift narrows the gap with recent Street target cuts in the US$40 to US$50 range. Analysts are pairing these lower targets with a mix of caution and recalibration, updating their models after the latest Q4 report while keeping the story anchored in earnings. Read on to see how you can track this evolving narrative and what to watch as new research comes through. Analyst Price Targets...

headline: JPMorgan Reprices AI Exposed Software Loans While Shares Screen As Undervalued
summary: JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) has tightened lending standards for certain borrowers in its private credit portfolios. Over the past two days, the bank marked down software related loans used as collateral, citing rising credit quality concerns linked to artificial intelligence. The move reflects a reassessment of risk in software focused private credit exposures that could be vulnerable to rapid AI driven disruption. For investors watching NYSE:JPM, this shift matters because private credit has...

headline: Retail Investors Hit Brakes Amid Uncertainty

headline: How The Ecolab (ECL) Narrative Is Shifting With New Price Targets And Ongoing Risks
summary: Price targets for Ecolab have recently clustered in the low to mid US$300s, while one fair value model holds steady at US$321.86. This gives you a clear reference point for how some analysts are framing upside and downside. Much of this activity is being driven by larger firms like JPMorgan and BofA nudging targets higher, even as others turn more cautious or move to Neutral. As you read on, you will see how these differing calls fit together and how you can track the story as it...
